name: Dewey Redman
abstract: Walter Dewey Redman (May 17, 1931 – September 2, 2006) was an American saxophonist who performed free jazz as a bandleader, and as a member of bands including those led by Ornette Coleman and Keith Jarrett. Redman mainly played tenor saxophone, though he occasionally also played alto, the Chinese suona (which he called a musette), and cl…

about_quotes.speaker: Nick Brignola
about_quotes.evidence: I played with Dewey Redman and I played some avante garde music.

about_quotes.speaker: Sheila Jordan
about_quotes.evidence: Cameron Brown mentions Dewey Redman as 'my wonderful band leader at the time' in the context of a project he organized, but Sheila Jordan does not describe a personal or professional relationship with him in her response.
about_quotes.doc_title: Sheila Jordan — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Lionel Hampton
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee only confirms the interviewer's statement that Dewey Redman is Josh Redman's father ('Yeah?') and does not describe any personal interaction or relationship with Dewey Redman himself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Lionel Hampton interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, October 18, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Kidd Jordan
about_quotes.evidence: I remember when Dewey Redman’s son came up Dewey was saying man my son is so and so and so.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kidd Jordan interviewed by Monk Rowe, Rome, New York, August 24, 2006

about_quotes.speaker: Carl Allen
about_quotes.evidence: one of the first recordings with Dewey Redman and Josh Redman together
about_quotes.doc_title: Carl Allen interviewed by Monk Rowe, Atlanta, Georgia, January 4, 2013
